title: Windows Editions, Requirements, Lifecycle, and Migration Decisions · Evidence Workshop
summary: A guided support bench extends Windows support and migration decision packet while retaining authorization, safety, baseline, privacy, accessibility, rollback, verification, documentation, and user communication. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of Windows support and migration decision packet while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare current Windows edition capabilities for identity, management, encryption, virtualization, remote access, updates, hardware, and licensing.
Evaluate processor, architecture, memory, storage, firmware, UEFI, Secure Boot capability, TPM, graphics, display, internet, and account requirements.
Treat Windows 10 exam knowledge as a dated legacy-support and migration case after its general support end, with current exception lookup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Windows Editions
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Unsupported Windows 10 devices and specialized apps create pressure to bypass Windows 11 eligibility without current lifecycle or recovery evidence. The workshop variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Windows Editions.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare current windows edition capabilities for identity, management, encryption, virtualization, remote access, updates, hardware, and licensing. For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Windows Editions, keep Windows Editions connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Unsupported Windows 10 devices and specialized apps create pressure to bypass Windows 11 eligibility without current lifecycle or recovery evidence. The workshop variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit.
